title: Home Repair Loan Programs
content:
# Home Repair Loan Programs

Learn about the home repair programs offered by the Office of Housing, whether or not you may qualify, and how to apply.

Toggle

Page Sections

The Mayor's Office of Housing, via the Boston Home Center, helps income-eligible homeowners with loans and technical assistance to repair their homes. Some loans are forgivable.

COMPLETE THE APPLICATION

Also available in Spanish (Español), Portuguese (Português), Chinese (中文), French (Français), Cape Verdean Creole (Kriolu Kabuverdianu), Haitian Creole (Kreyòl ayisyen), Somali (Soomaali), Russian (русский), Vietnamese (Tiếng Việt), and Arabic (عربى).

## Specific programs

Specific programs

HomeWorks Green

This program provides eligible homeowners with a 0% interest deferred loan up to $70,000 for green repairs and home improvements.

Homeowners may use the HomeWorks Green Loan to finance both interior and exterior repairs. At least 1/3 of the project scope must be used for eligible green repairs. Eligible green repairs include repairs that address energy efficiency, health and safety barriers, and clean energy technologies.

learn more about homeworks green

HomeWorks Home Equity Loan Program (HomeWorks HELP)

This program provides eligible homeowners with a 0% interest rate, deferred payment home repair loan up to $50,000.

The amount offered through this program differs depending on the type of home and the owner’s qualifications. Your income needs to be at or below 135% of the Area Median Income (AMI) as indicated by the HUD Income Limits . Applicants between 120-135% AMI must provide an even dollar for dollar match.

* $20,000 for condominiums owners
* $30,000 for single family homes
* $40,000 for two family homes, and
* $50,000 for three and four family homes

Apply for HomeWorks HELP

Senior Home Repair

This program is for 62-year-old and older owner-occupant homeowners.

This is a 0% interest, deferred payment home repair loan for everything from minor repairs to replacements of heating systems. Loan amounts are determined by the necessity of health and safety repairs.

This program is available to owner-occupant homeowners of 1-4 family homes, who are at least 62 years of age, and earn less than 80% of Area Median Income (AMI) as indicated by the HUD Income Limits.

Apply for Senior Home Repair

LeadSafe Boston

This program offers a forgivable loan of up to $12,000 per housing unit to mitigate lead paint.

$12,000 per housing unit may be available to homeowners to mitigate lead paint, along with technical help to ensure compliance with Massachusetts’ Lead Law. The following may qualify for Leadsafe Boston:

* **Owner-occupants** of 1–4 unit homes or condos earning at or below 80% of Community Development Block Grant Income (CDBG) as indicated by the HUD Income Limits
* **Investor owners** of 1–4 unit buildings must prove that at least 50% of the building's residents are low- or moderate-income. Remaining units must be occupied by families with an income at 80% or below of CDBG Moderate Income.

Apply for Leadsafe Boston

Seniors Save

This program provides senior homeowners with up to $12,000 to replace aging heating systems.

This program is for senior homeowners aged 60 years and older who earn less than 80% of Area Median Income (AMI) as indicated by the HUD Income Limits  There is an $12,000 grant to help eligible homeowners replace their aging heating systems. There is also a 0% interest deferred loan for the balance of the replacement. Heating systems that have already been replaced are **not eligible** for the program. Seniors Save is not a reimbursement program.

Apply for Seniors Save
